text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon, little Lily cherished a bright red berry. Every spot where it touched seemed like a tiny bloom. She wanted that beauty to last forever. One starry night, she sat under the old oak tree. With eyes closed, she whispered dreams of the berry.

The next dawn, the berry wasn't there. Lily was devastate. That very evening, a gentle old owl hooted. "Oh," sighed Lily. "You discovered it."

She opened her eyes. On the ground lay a silken sack. Inside lay dozens of red berries.

"Thank you so much," she murmured. Those weren't just any berries. They were ancient ones, whispered about in legends.

With tears drying into shiny spots on her nose, Lily listened. Somewhere deep, an owl hooted again.

That's when the world changed a little. From then on, the woods echoed stories Lily hadn't known, learned through stars and owls. She grew into an
